root_text.an3.130:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an3.130:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā anuruddho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.130:1.3: “idhāhaṁ, āvuso sāriputta, dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena sahassaṁ lokaṁ olokemi.
root_text.an3.130:1.4: Āraddhaṁ kho pana me vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ.
root_text.an3.130:1.5: Atha ca pana me nānupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuccatī”ti.
root_text.an3.130:2.1: “Yaṁ kho te, āvuso anuruddha, evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an3.130:2.2: ‘ahaṁ dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena sahassaṁ lokaṁ volokemī’ti, idaṁ te mānasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.130:2.3: Yampi te, āvuso anuruddha, evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an3.130:2.4: ‘āraddhaṁ kho pana me vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā, passaddho kāyo asāraddho, samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggan’ti, idaṁ te uddhaccasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.130:2.5: Yampi te, āvuso anuruddha, evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an3.130:2.6: ‘atha ca pana me nānupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuccatī’ti, idaṁ te kukkuccasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.130:2.7: Sādhu vatāyasmā anuruddho ime tayo dhamme pahāya, ime tayo dhamme amanasikaritvā amatāya dhātuyā cittaṁ upasaṁharatū”ti.
root_text.an3.130:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho aparena samayena ime tayo dhamme pahāya, ime tayo dhamme amanasikaritvā amatāya dhātuyā cittaṁ upasaṁhari.
root_text.an3.130:3.2: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto viharanto nacirasseva—yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihāsi.
root_text.an3.130:3.3: “Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā”ti abbhaññāsi.
root_text.an3.130:3.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā anuruddho arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.an3.130:1.1: Then Venerable Anuruddha went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an3.130:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to him:
translation_text.an3.130:1.3: “Here’s the thing, Reverend Sāriputta. With clairvoyance that is purified and surpasses the human, I survey the thousandfold galaxy.
translation_text.an3.130:1.4: My energy is roused up and unflagging, my mindfulness is established and lucid, my body is tranquil and undisturbed, and my mind is immersed in samādhi and unified.
translation_text.an3.130:1.5: But my mind is not freed from the defilements by not grasping.”
translation_text.an3.130:2.2: ‘With clairvoyance that is purified and surpasses the human, I survey the thousandfold galaxy,’ that’s your conceit.
translation_text.an3.130:2.4: ‘My energy is roused up and unflagging, my mindfulness is established and lucid, my body is tranquil and undisturbed, and my mind is immersed in samādhi,’ that’s your restlessness.
translation_text.an3.130:2.6: ‘But my mind is not freed from the defilements by not grasping,’ that’s your remorse.
translation_text.an3.130:2.7: It would be good to give up these three things. Ignore them and apply your mind to freedom from death.”
translation_text.an3.130:3.1: After some time Anuruddha gave up these three things. Ignoring them, he applied his mind to freedom from death.
translation_text.an3.130:3.2: Then Anuruddha, living alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute, soon realized the supreme culmination of the spiritual path in this very life. He lived having achieved with his own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.an3.130:3.3: He understood: “Rebirth is ended; the spiritual journey has been completed; what had to be done has been done; there is nothing further for this place.”
translation_text.an3.130:3.4: And Venerable Anuruddha became one of the perfected.
